zh.Desmond, Kerry, Munster, Ireland: 愛爾蘭蒙斯特迪 本 克里
zh.Lixnaw, Kerry, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 克里萊希 斯納哈
zh.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw於1255年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 克里。他和Baroness Elizabeth de Canton至少有2個女兒。他去世於1363年在愛爾蘭庫迪 克里萊希 斯納哈享年108歲。

zh-hans.Desmond, Kerry, Munster, Ireland: 爱尔兰蒙斯特迪 本 克里
zh-hans.Lixnaw, Kerry, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 克里萊希 斯納哈
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw于1255年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 克里。他和Baroness Elizabeth de Canton至少有2个女儿。他去世于1363年在爱尔兰庫迪 克里萊希 斯納哈享年108岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzMaurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzmorris
meanings: Irish: variant of   Fitzmaurice  .

nameMeanings.name: VIth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Pasquale, Angelo, Carmine, Constantino, Gennaro, Giuseppe, Leonardo, Lia, Nunzio, Remo, Salvatore.

Italian: from the personal name Vito, Latin Vitus ‘lively, energetic’, from vita ‘life’ (compare Vita ). The name was popular in the Middle Ages as the result of the cult of an early Christian martyr in southern Italy, about whom very little is known. He was regarded as a patron against epilepsy and the nervous tremor named after him, ‘St. Vitus's dance’. His cult spread into Germany and thence through Eastern Europe, where the name was reinforced by native Slavic names such as Vitoslav and Vitomir, with the first element most likely derived from Old Slavic vitъ ‘loot, wealth’ (see also Wit ). It may also derive in part from the Langobardic name Wido or Frankish Wit(t)o (see Guido ).

variants.name: Viti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Guido, Rocco, Stefano, Attilio, Benedetto, Enzo, Federico, Giulio, Michelangelo, Pio, Sal.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name   Vito  .
In some cases also an Italianized form of Slovenian   Vitez  .

variants.name: Veit
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Siegbert, Alois, Dieter, Eberhardt, Fritz, Manferd, Theodor.
German: from the personal name Veit (from Latin Vitus; see Vito ).
Americanized form of German Veith , a variant of 1, written thus to preserve the German pronunciation of th as t.

variants.name: Vita
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Alfredo, Angelo, Domenic, Mario, Orlando, Salvatore, Angelina, Carmelo, Carmine, Clementina, Francesco, Giovanni, Leonardo, Ottavio, Santo.  Spanish Edgardo, Eleazar, Ines, Juan, Manuel.
Italian and Spanish: from a female personal name, from Latin and Italian vita ‘life’.
Italian: from a short form of a personal name formed with the element vita (see 1 above), such as the omen or well-wishing names Bellavita and Bonavita .

variants.name: Vidovic
meanings: Croatian and Serbian (Vidović); Slovenian and Slovak (Vidovič): patronymic from the personal name Vid, a South Slavic form of Latin Vitus (see Vito ). As a Croatian and Serbian patronymic it may also be from a short form of Slavic compound names such as Vidoslav, based on the element vid ‘sight’.

variants.name: Vitek
meanings: Czech (Vítek): from a pet form of the personal name Vít, Latin Vitus (see Vito ). Saint Vitus or Vít is the patron saint of Prague.

variants.name: Vitti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dino, Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Carmel, Donato, Giovanni, Levio, Luciano, Luigi, Oreste, Ornella.

Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Vitto, which can be a variant of Vito or a short form of a personal name ending in -vitto, such as Iacovitto, Salvitto, or a name of ancient Germanic origin, from Witto (see Guy 1).

variants.name: Vitello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Baldassare, Carmelo, Ciro, Salvatore, Vincenzo.
Italian:
from a pet form of the personal name   Vito  .

variants.name: Vidas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Latvian Arturs, Ilmars, Ojars.
Croatian: from an old vernacular pet form of the personal name Vid (from Latin Vitus; see Vito ) or of Slavic compound names such as Vidoslav, based on the element vid ‘sight’.
Lithuanian and Latvian: from the personal name Vidas, a derivative of Latin Vitus ‘lively, energetic’ (see Vito ).

variants.name: Vietti
meanings: Italian: patronymic or plural form of a pet form of the personal name Vio, which is a northern variant of Vito .

variants.name: Vitolo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Dante, Gennaro, Salvatore, Antonio, Caterina, Franco, Marco, Nunzio, Rino, Stefano.
Italian (Campania): from a pet form of   Vito  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Kerry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the rare Middle English personal name Kari (Old Norse Kári).
Irish: from Ó Ciardha, ‘descendant of Ciardha’, a personal name meaning ‘the black one’.

variants.name: Carey
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ciardha, a midland surname meaning ‘descendant of Ciardha’, a personal name derived from ciar ‘dark, black’. See Kerry .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhiachra ‘son of Fiachra’.

English: from Carey Barton in Devon or Castle Cary, Lytes Cary, Cary Fitzpaine, or Babcary, all in Somerset, named for the rivers on which they stand; both river names probably derive from the Celtic root car- ‘love, liking’, perhaps with the meaning ‘pleasant stream’.

variants.name: Kerrison
meanings: English (Norfolk): from the rare Middle English personal name Kari, Kare (see Kerry ) + -son.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Maurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Mauricius, a derivative of Maurus (a byname meaning ‘Moor’, i.e. ‘dark, swarthy’), borne by, among others, an early Byzantine emperor ( c.539–602 ). It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was popular in the Middle English period, but was not widely adopted by the nobility and became rare in the 17th century. Between the mid-19th century and the 1940s, it was moderately popular but has since faded again. See also Morris .

nameMeanings.name: FitzJohn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name John , ‘son of John’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

variants.name: Fidgeon
meanings: 1  English: (i) variant of   Vivian  .  Compare  Videan  . (ii) variant of   Fitzjohn  .
2 Irish: reduced variant of Mag Guagáin, see McGuigan .

en.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw was born in 1255, in County Kerry, Ireland. He had at least 2 daughters with Baroness Elizabeth de Canton. He died in 1363, in Leic Snámha, County Kerry, Ireland, at the age of 108.

fr.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw est né en 1255, à County Kerry, Irlande. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Baroness Elizabeth de Canton. Il est décédé en 1363, à Leic Snámha, County Kerry, Irlande, à l’âge de 108 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw wurde 1255 in County Kerry, Irland geboren. Er hatte mit Baroness Elizabeth de Canton mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1363 im Alter von 108 Jahren in Leic Snámha, County Kerry, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw è nato il nel 1255 nel County Kerry, Irlanda. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Baroness Elizabeth de Canton. Morì nel 1363, nel Leic Snámha, County Kerry, Irlanda, all'età di 108 anni.

ja.Desmond, Kerry, Munster, Ireland: アイルランドムンスターディオセゼ オフ ケリー
ja.Kerry, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ ケリー
ja.Lixnaw, Kerry, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ ケリーレイック スンaムハ
ja.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnawは1255年にアイルランドコウントイ ケリーで生まれた。彼はBaroness Elizabeth de Cantonとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1363年にアイルランドコウントイ ケリーレイック スンaムハで108歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw(은)는 1255년 아일랜드 카운티 케리에서 태어났다. 그는 Baroness Elizabeth de Canton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1363년 아일랜드 카운티 케리 레이치 스a하에서 108세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw nasceu em 1255, em County Kerry, Irlanda. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Baroness Elizabeth de Canton. Ele faleceu em 1363, em Leic Snámha, County Kerry, Irlanda, com 108 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw родился в 1255 г. в местности коунти кирри, Ирландия. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Baroness Elizabeth de Canton. Он умер в 1363 г. в местности лик снaмха, коунти кирри, Ирландия в возрасте 108 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzJohn FitzMaurice VIth Lord Kerry and Lixnaw nació en 1255, en County Kerry, Irlanda. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Baroness Elizabeth de Canton. Murió en 1363, en Leic Snámha, County Kerry, Irlanda, a la edad de 108 años.
